Company Description LawFlow Automation is a growing organization focused on optimizing legal and operational workflows through technology and automation. The company designs and implements solutions that streamline repetitive tasks, reduce manual errors, and improve overall process efficiency. By collaborating closely with legal and business teams, LawFlow Automation builds tools that support better decision-making and faster execution. Team members are encouraged to propose innovative ideas, take ownership of their work, and contribute to continuous improvement. The environment supports learning, experimentation, and the use of modern automation platforms and best practices.Role Description: The Automation Consultant is a full-time, on-site role based in the Lima Metropolitan Area. This role is responsible for designing, building, and maintaining automated workflows that support legal, administrative, and operational processes. Day-to-day tasks include gathering requirements from stakeholders, mapping current processes, configuring or scripting automation tools, and integrating different systems and data sources. The Automation Specialist will create and maintain test cases, execute test cycles, and collaborate with quality assurance to validate automation reliability and performance. The role also involves monitoring automated processes, troubleshooting issues, performing root-cause analysis, and documenting solutions and technical procedures. In addition, the Automation Specialist will support training and knowledge transfer, helping colleagues adopt and effectively use new automated solutions.QualificationsStrong skills in Process Automation and Automation, with experience implementing end-to-end workflows in business or legal environments.Proficiency in troubleshooting automated processes, identifying defects, and performing root-cause analysis on system or integration issues.Experience designing and executing Test Cases, including regression tests and validation scenarios for automated solutions.Knowledge of Quality Assurance practices, including test planning, defect tracking, and collaboration with QA or engineering teams.Familiarity with automation platforms or scripting languages (e.g., RPA tools, low-code/no-code platforms, or similar).Ability to document workflows, technical configurations, and standard operating procedures clearly and accurately.Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail and a focus on reliability and scalability.Effective communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively with technical and non-technical stakeholders on-site.Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.Key ResponsibilitiesProcess Discovery: Analyzing day-to-day operations to pinpoint bottlenecks and identify tasks suitable for automation.Workflow Design & Integration: Connecting different business apps (like CRMs, HR systems, and email marketing tools) using platforms like Zapier, Axiom, and no-code.AI Implementation: Building chatbots, AI agents, and Generative AI systems to handle customer queries or internal documentation. Testing & Maintenance: Troubleshooting broken workflows, monitoring performance data, and tweaking logic to improve conversion or response rates.

Role OverviewWe are looking for a proactive and detail-oriented Business Analyst to bridge the gap between business needs and technology solutions. The candidate will work closely with stakeholders to gather requirements, analyze processes, and support the delivery of high-quality solutions.Experience in Terminal Operating Systems (TOS) or industries such as shipping, logistics, ports, or terminal operations will be a strong advantage.Key ResponsibilitiesGather, analyze, and document business and functional requirements Work closely with stakeholders to understand business processes and objectives Prepare BRDs, FRDs, user stories, and use cases Collaborate with development and QA teams to ensure accurate implementation Participate in requirement walkthroughs, sprint planning, and backlog grooming Support UAT (User Acceptance Testing) and ensure requirements are met Identify process improvements and recommend solutions Act as a liaison between business users and technical teams Track project progress and assist in issue resolution Ensure documentation is maintained and up to date Required Skills & Qualifications3+ years of experience as a Business Analyst in IT projects Strong knowledge of requirement gathering and documentation techniques Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments Good understanding of SDLC processes Proficiency in tools like JIRA, Confluence, MS Excel, Visio, or similar Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities Ability to handle multiple tasks and priorities Preferred / Domain Expertise (Highly Desirable)Experience with Terminal Operating Systems (TOS) Exposure to shipping, logistics, port operations, or terminal systems
